name: Certbot
description: Certbot — EFFs free tool to automatically obtain and renew Lets Encrypt TLS certificates.

seo.title: Certbot — Secrets Management
seo.meta_description: Certbot: Certbot — EFFs free tool to automatically obtain and renew Lets Encrypt TLS certificates.

when_to_use: Auto-renewal of Let's Encrypt TLS certs via cron
Wildcard certificate issuance with DNS-01 ACME challenge
HTTPS enforcement for self-hosted web applications
CI/CD cert automation for staging environment security
Certbot certonly for custom web server TLS configurations

pros: Free TLS certs via Let's Encrypt automation
Auto-renewal with cron or systemd timers
Wide plugin support: Nginx, Apache, DNS
cons: Rate limits hurt during misconfigured retries
No built-in cert inventory or dashboard
review.verdict: The default way to automate Let's Encrypt on a single host; less ideal once you're juggling dozens.
review.best_for: Admins running a handful of Linux servers needing free, renewable TLS.
review.not_for: Enterprises needing centralized cert lifecycle management across thousands of endpoints.
